title: Amazon Women In The Mood

jokes.text: So has Fry. What's the big deal?
jokes.setup: Concern about Nibbler coughing up hairballs
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that Fry also coughs up hairballs
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - only Fry would casually admit to coughing up hairballs. Great economy and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: He's got me beat.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about Fry coughing up hairballs
jokes.punchline: Fry admits Nibbler is better at it than him
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that escalates the absurdity by treating hairball production as a competition.

jokes.text: Still, I propose we make Zoidberg do it. All in favor? All opposed? All abstaining?
jokes.setup: Democratic proposal to clean up together
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests making Zoidberg do all the work through fake vote
jokes.explanation: Good satire of workplace dynamics and fake democracy. The formal voting structure makes the cruelty funnier.

jokes.text: He's dead. He always seemed so full of life. Laughing, singing, begging for scraps. And now this!
jokes.setup: Dramatic mourning for seemingly dead Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: List of 'life' includes pathetic 'begging for scraps'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where the 'celebration of life' includes increasingly pathetic details. Solid structure.

jokes.text: Why with all the crying? So, that's where I left my shell.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg is fine, just molted his shell
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Zoidberg obliviousness. The casual 'so that's where I left my shell' is beautifully understated.

jokes.text: I didn't know it came off. It was getting a little cramped, so I molted, why not?
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's casual 'why not?' attitude about molting
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with Zoidberg's alien biology treated casually. The 'why not?' is perfectly Zoidberg.

jokes.text: The fresh air feels good. - Stop doing that!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg enjoying being out of his shell
jokes.punchline: He's apparently doing something visually disturbing
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that works through implication and Leela's disgusted reaction

jokes.text: Those were toenail clippings. A feast is a feast.
jokes.punchline: They're toenail clippings, but Zoidberg doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting Zoidberg moment with great economy. 'A feast is a feast' is quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Did you swallow your phone again?
jokes.setup: Fry's phone ringing from inside him
jokes.punchline: Implies this has happened before ('again')
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that establishes this as recurring Fry behavior. The 'again' sells it.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. You're crying like a woman.
jokes.setup: Zapp offering to help with Kif's problems
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults Kif for showing emotion
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp - immediately contradicts his offer to help. Good character consistency but predictable.

jokes.text: Oh, that's rich! Go on.
jokes.punchline: Zapp finds the idea of Kif in love hilarious
jokes.explanation: Solid Zapp cruelty moment. His dismissive attitude toward Kif's feelings is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yes, it was in all the papers.
jokes.setup: Reference to Zapp piloting ship into black hole
jokes.punchline: Zapp casually acknowledging his massive failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - completely unconcerned about his catastrophic incompetence. Good deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Then let the negotiations begin. I propose we go out on 10 dates. How about zero? Nine. Zero. Seven? Eight?
jokes.setup: Zapp wanting to negotiate a double date
jokes.punchline: Absurd 'negotiation' where he keeps lowering his own offer
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity as Zapp negotiates against himself. Good structure and rhythm.

jokes.text: Five, and that's my final offer. Four! One. Two. Half. I'll take it.
jokes.punchline: Zapp keeps changing his 'final offer' and accepts 'half'
jokes.explanation: Strong continuation of the negotiation bit. Zapp's desperation and incompetence shine through.

jokes.text: Five, and that's my final offer. Four! - One. - Two. - Half. - I'll take it.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to half a date, with Zapp immediately accepting despite it being worse than his last offer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to the absurd concept of 'half a date' with perfect pacing and character logic

jokes.text: We'll meet you for part of dinner and half of a movie.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'half a date'
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the negotiation. The literal interpretation is both clever and absurd.

jokes.text: Wrong, wrong, wrong. And what's that? Candy? Candy's for dorks. Give me that.
jokes.setup: Zapp giving dating advice to Kif
jokes.punchline: Rejects flowers and candy as dorky, then takes the candy
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp hypocrisy moment - calls candy dorky then immediately wants it. Character-consistent.

jokes.text: Well, well. This looks to be one disturbingly erotic date.
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately makes it weird and sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp inappropriate behavior. Predictable but well-executed character moment.

jokes.text: Half-date. Half-bottle. Quarter-shell. He's not hungry. It's expensive.
jokes.punchline: Leela literally enforcing half portions of everything
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with good escalation. Each cut becomes more ridiculous (quarter-shell).

jokes.text: And I'd like two steaks, and the ladies will have sensual salads with low-cal sensual dressing.
jokes.punchline: Makes salads 'sensual' for no reason
jokes.explanation: Typical Zapp creepiness. The repetition of 'sensual' is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: If I said you had a beautiful body, would you take your pants off and dance?
jokes.setup: Kif finally trying to talk to Amy
jokes.punchline: Uses Zapp's terrible pickup line verbatim
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Kif following Zapp's terrible advice. The contrast with shy Kif makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I find the most erotic part of a woman is the boobies.
jokes.setup: Kif continuing to use pickup lines
jokes.punchline: Childish word 'boobies' in 'sophisticated' observation
jokes.explanation: The juvenile 'boobies' in a pretentiously formatted line is funny. Classic Futurama crude-but-innocent humor.

jokes.text: This half-date is entirely over. Amy? Conference.
jokes.setup: Kif's terrible pickup lines ruining the date
jokes.punchline: Leela calls it off, referencing the half-date concept
jokes.explanation: Good callback that pays off the half-date setup while escalating the disaster

jokes.text: There's one way into a woman's heart and parts beyond. I speak, of course, of karaoke.
jokes.setup: Zapp building up to romantic advice about reaching women's hearts
jokes.punchline: The solution is karaoke
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect Zapp logic. The formal 'I speak, of course' makes the karaoke reveal funnier.

jokes.text: Let me show you why they call me 'The Velour Fog.'
jokes.punchline: Self-appointed nickname combining velour and smooth jazz reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp nickname that captures his self-delusion and velour obsession. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: She's built like a steak house, but she handles like a bistro.
jokes.punchline: Describes vehicle performance in restaurant terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist line that perfectly fits the restaurant-ship concept. Highly quotable with great economy and originality.

jokes.text: She's out of control! - You win again, gravity!
jokes.punchline: Zapp personifies gravity as his nemesis
jokes.explanation: Great Zapp line that deflects responsibility by treating physics as personal vendetta

jokes.text: Hey, gringos, here comes El Zoido to ruin your drinking water!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying on a Mexican-themed shell
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical Mexican bandit character with water pollution threat
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotype humor that feels cheap and dated. Low craft score for lazy writing.

jokes.text: I'm Mexican, and I find that offensive!
jokes.punchline: Bender claims to be Mexican and offended
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the previous joke's offensiveness. Bender claiming Mexican identity is absurd but appropriate.

jokes.text: You Latins are so hot-blooded.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg doubles down with another stereotype
jokes.explanation: Another stereotype-based joke that feels lazy. Zoidberg's obliviousness is in character but the humor is cheap.

jokes.text: The number you have dialed has crashed into a planet.
jokes.punchline: Phone company has specific message for crashed restaurants
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi comedy that imagines how phone systems would handle space travel disasters. Great economy.

jokes.text: Bender, think of the señoritas! Vámonos!
jokes.punchline: Appeal to Bender's programmed attraction to women
jokes.explanation: Good character motivation understanding. The Spanish callback to Bender's Mexican claim is nice.

jokes.text: Well, nothing to do but repopulate the human race, just me and you. And maybe you.
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately thinking of reproduction, including Amy as afterthought
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp inappropriate response to crisis. The 'and maybe you' afterthought for Amy is nicely dismissive.

jokes.text: Me hear people, but me not see. Everybody make mistake.
jokes.setup: Amazon women speaking in primitive grammar
jokes.punchline: Philosophical acceptance delivered in broken English
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for the Amazons. The broken English with wisdom is a nice contrast.

jokes.text: Well, hello. I usually don't say this, but you are the most beautiful trio of gigantic ladies I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Zapp trying to charm the Amazon women
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment calling them gigantic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - trying to be suave but insulting. The structure of the compliment-insult is well done.

jokes.text: I find the most erotic part of a woman is the boo...
jokes.setup: Zapp using the same pickup line as Kif
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off before completing 'boobies'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Zapp uses the same lines. The cutoff creates anticipation for viewers who know the line.

jokes.text: Oh, your God!
jokes.setup: Seeing evidence of the men's capture
jokes.punchline: Bender's atheist 'Oh, your God' instead of 'Oh, my God'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment. The atheist robot's version of the exclamation is clever and character-consistent.

jokes.text: Oh, your God!
jokes.punchline: Bender's atheist variation of 'Oh my God'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific wordplay that perfectly reflects Bender's atheism in natural exclamation

jokes.text: They were probably nagged to death. Are you with me, fellas?
jokes.setup: Learning men died out on Amazon planet
jokes.punchline: Fry makes sexist joke to female captors
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's social obliviousness but relies on tired sexist humor trope. Character-appropriate but low craft.

jokes.text: No can dunk, but good fundamentals. That more fun to watch.
jokes.punchline: Earnest defense of fundamentals over flashy plays
jokes.explanation: Good sports humor that gently mocks the common criticism of women's basketball. The earnest delivery sells it.

jokes.text: This our comedy club. Humor different here. It not reinforce stereotypes. Comedy come from character, not abstract craziness.
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on comedy writing philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about comedy writing that describes Futurama's own approach. Self-referential without being precious.

jokes.text: Translation: boring.
jokes.setup: Amazon explanation of their sophisticated comedy
jokes.punchline: Zapp's dismissive, crude response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp response - anti-intellectual and dismissive. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Is she hot? Not important. She all-knowing. In other words, no.
jokes.setup: Amazon deflecting question about leader's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Zapp interprets 'not important' as confirmation she's ugly
jokes.explanation: Great structure with Zapp's perfect misinterpretation. Shows his shallow priorities and poor logic.

jokes.text: It different kind of politician. Not beltway insider.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they follow a computer leader
jokes.punchline: Uses modern political terminology about outsider candidates
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about outsider candidates. The anachronistic language from primitive Amazons is funny.

jokes.text: That does not fem-pute.
jokes.punchline: Gendered version of 'compute'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that establishes the computer's feminist programming. Simple but effective character-building.

jokes.text: You mean snu-snu!
jokes.setup: Discussion about what men are used for
jokes.punchline: Excited recognition of euphemistic term
jokes.explanation: Introduction of 'snu-snu' - a brilliant euphemism that became iconic. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: We hear tell men used for snu-snu. All we have go on are ancient legend and subscription to Cosmo.
jokes.setup: Amazons' knowledge about men and snu-snu
jokes.punchline: Ancient legends and Cosmopolitan magazine as equal sources of wisdom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of mythical knowledge sources with modern women's magazine

jokes.text: FYI, I could be used for snu-snu.
jokes.punchline: Fry volunteers himself awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's desperation and social awkwardness

jokes.text: What did they die of? Crushed pelvises.
jokes.setup: Question about how previous men died
jokes.punchline: Death by sexual activity with giant women
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and absurd. Perfect setup/punchline structure with incredible economy. Became a cultural reference.

jokes.text: What did they die of? - Crushed pelvises.
jokes.punchline: Death by excessive snu-snu
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - takes male fantasy to logical deadly conclusion with medical precision

jokes.text: Yes! Thank you, Lord in heaven!
jokes.punchline: Celebrating despite the fatal outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reaction - prioritizing sex over survival. Dark comedy with great character consistency.

jokes.text: Fem-puter sentences them to death... by snu-snu!
jokes.punchline: Death method is what they actually want
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic structure that subverts expectations. The pause before 'by snu-snu' is comedically perfect.

jokes.text: ...by snu-snu! - Yeah! - What are you, gay?
jokes.punchline: Fry celebrates, Zapp questions his sexuality for being excited
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the absurd reversal of death being celebrated

jokes.text: What are you, gay?
jokes.setup: Kif showing fear about death by snu-snu
jokes.punchline: Zapp questions his sexuality for not wanting it
jokes.explanation: Typical Zapp crudeness but relies on lazy homophobic humor. Character-appropriate but low craft.

jokes.text: But the snu-snu part will be good. - Baby, it'll blow your mind.
jokes.punchline: Focus on the positive aspects of fatal punishment
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy showing Amazons' casual attitude toward lethal sex

jokes.text: They make fun women's basketball. What?! Did you explain how good fundamentals make up... for their inability to dunk?
jokes.punchline: Basketball mockery is unforgivable offense
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing what truly matters to this society, perfect callback integration

jokes.text: He big jerk like man. Sure, but check the crotch. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be a robot, not a man
jokes.punchline: Physical verification of his lack of male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good logical resolution to Bender's situation with appropriate physical comedy. The delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I never thought I'd die like this, but I'd always really hoped.
jokes.punchline: Perfect contradiction - never thought but always hoped
jokes.explanation: Near-perfect line with brilliant logical contradiction. Became one of Futurama's most quoted lines. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: Then the large women. Then the petite women. Then the large women again.
jokes.punchline: Excessive rotation ensuring death
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes clear this is definitely fatal while maintaining comedic rhythm

jokes.text: Then the large women again.
jokes.punchline: Additional round with the most dangerous group
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Kif's 'reward' increasingly dangerous. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Really? Honest and true? - Yes. And that person calling and hanging up was me.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was the mysterious caller
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution with emotional payoff, ties together earlier subplot

jokes.text: It Og's first time. Be gentle!
jokes.punchline: Concerned Amazon asking for gentleness
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from role reversal and Amazon's inexperience with the execution method

jokes.text: Maybe you can interface with my ass. By biting it!
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Bender interface with the fem-puter
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature insult using computer terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line combining his signature insult with tech terminology. Great character integration and quotability.

jokes.text: It time snu-snu! - Can't we just cuddle?
jokes.punchline: Fry's exhausted plea for gentleness
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal joke with exhausted Fry wanting intimacy over sex

jokes.text: I'm a scary fem-puter. Release the prisoners. And bring gold. Lots of gold. - Why Fem-puter want gold? - You heard the man.
jokes.punchline: Bender's greed shows through the disguise
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender can't help revealing his true nature even while saving everyone

jokes.text: You're no fem-puter, you're a fembot.
jokes.setup: Belief that they're dealing with a computer
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's actually a robot like Bender
jokes.explanation: Solid plot revelation with good economy. Sets up the backstory explanation that follows.

jokes.text: Have you any idea how it feels to be a fembot in a manbot's man-puter's world?
jokes.punchline: Overwrought feminist rhetoric using bot/computer terminology
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that parodies feminist rhetoric while maintaining the robot theme. Gets a bit wordy.

jokes.text: The spirit is willing, but the flesh is spongy and bruised.
jokes.punchline: Biblical reference adapted to their physical state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant adaptation of biblical quote to the situation. Perfect economy and became highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm a scary fem-puter. Release the prisoners. And bring gold. Lots of gold.
jokes.setup: Bender impersonating the fem-puter to help friends
jokes.punchline: Can't resist asking for gold for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - even when helping friends, he can't resist personal gain. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I found it in the same dumpster, but this one had a live raccoon in it.
jokes.punchline: The upgrade is that it came with a live animal
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg callback with perfect character logic - a live raccoon is an improvement. Absurd but character-consistent.

jokes.text: All I know is, this gold says that was the best mission ever.
jokes.punchline: Only cares about the gold he acquired
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Bender judges everything by personal gain. Solid callback to his gold demand.

tmdb_overview: A double-date for Kif, Amy, Zapp, and Leela ends in disaster when their orbiting restaurant crashes on planet Amazonia. The hulking female inhabitants of the planet take their male captives to the omniscient Femputer, who orders Fry, Zapp, and Kif to death by "snu-snu".
